title: Fix the Notice Headings
statement_md:
The cultural committee typed up the notice-board headings in a rush, and the caps lock key had a mind of its own — every heading came out in messy mixed case.

Before the notices go up, tidy each heading: every word should begin with a capital letter, and the rest of the word should be in small letters. The words themselves and the single spaces between them stay exactly where they are.

For each heading, print the cleaned-up version.
input_format_md:
The first line contains a single integer `T` — the number of headings.

Each of the next `T` lines contains one full heading: one or more words made of English letters (mixed upper and lower case), separated by single spaces, with no leading or trailing spaces. Read each heading as a whole line — the words on one line belong to the same heading.
output_format_md: For each heading, print the cleaned-up heading on its own line.
constraints_md:
- `1 ≤ T ≤ 10`
- each heading has at least one word; words contain only English letters (`A`–`Z`, `a`–`z`)
- words are separated by exactly one space; no leading or trailing spaces
- each heading is at most `2 × 10^4` characters long

reference_solution.python:
import sys

def main():
    lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    t = int(lines[0])
    out = []
    for i in range(1, t + 1):
        out.append(" ".join(w.capitalize() for w in lines[i].split(" ")))
    print("\n".join(out))

main()

hints: Read each heading as a full line, not word by word — the words on one line belong together. Then ask: what decides whether a letter should be capital? Only whether it is the first letter of its word.

Either split the line on spaces and rebuild each word as first-letter-uppercase plus rest-lowercase, or walk the line once with a flag: at the start of the line and right after every space, the next letter goes uppercase; every other letter goes lowercase.

sample_tests.input:
2
fRESHERS wELCOME pARTY
exam TIME table CHANGED
sample_tests.output:
Freshers Welcome Party
Exam Time Table Changed
